mechanism_narrative: Elevated FEDFUNDS level restricts liquidity and raises discount rates, pressuring equity valuations downward over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >55% of months when FEDFUNDS > 4% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point rise in UNRATE over three months signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending expectations and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly negative (mean > -1%) in triggered instances over a 5-year window, or hit rate < 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with restrictive monetary policy signals an overheating economy where the Fed will tighten further, producing negative SPX returns as terminal rate fears rise.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth positive and FEDFUNDS > 4.0%
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS has zero or positive rank correlation with 20d SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals contracting supply-side output. In a high-inflation regime where CPIAUCSL is elevated, this contraction is stagflationary and drives negative SPX returns as margins compress.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in >50% of triggered instances (INDPRO decline alongside CPIAUCSL > 300) over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect robust construction activity and forward-looking residential investment, which drives positive demand expectations and SPX returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST level changes have non-significant correlation (p > 0.10) with SPX 60d forward returns over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds a threshold, persistent inflation forces the Fed to maintain higher rates for longer, depressing equity multiples and generating negative 5-day returns on inflation surprise.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are not negative in >60% of months when CPIAUCSL > 300 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth alongside elevated retail sales confirms a consumer-demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues and drives positive SPX returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to produce positive rank IC (>0.03) with SPX 20d returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO below its recent trend signals a negative supply shock that reduces earnings visibility and drives negative SPX returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.
